author: codex-agent

patch.value: Tracks pm-lvej, pm-6fmt, and pm-8ys5 by aligning linked-test sandbox behavior with normal workspace command semantics and preventing new unrunnable linked-test metadata.
Linked-test parity and runnable command enforcement
Deliver linked-test sandbox parity for extension-defined custom types and enforce runnable linked-test command metadata without regressing existing repositories.
Linked tests preserve project/global extension parity for custom types and new linked-test entries require command metadata while legacy malformed entries remain safely handled at runtime.
Dedupe checks completed and implementation/test/doc scope identified.
Eliminate linked-test parity and runnable-metadata drift
Prevents contradictory automation evidence for custom-type repositories
Improves trust in pm test and pm test-all governance evidence
Consistent type-filter behavior and runnable linked-test metadata
User-reported regressions show divergence between interactive and linked-test automation contexts.
Run pm list/search with custom type directly then via pm test linked command and compare filter acceptance.
Linked automation and workspace commands accept equivalent custom type filters and linked tests are runnable.
Linked sandbox rejects extension-defined types and path-only linked tests are accepted but skipped.
Teams may trust incomplete linked-test evidence and get false negatives for valid custom-type filters.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Created after dedupe checks confirmed no active item for pm-lvej pm-6fmt pm-8ys5 scope.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Implement docs-first with unit integration and regression verification.
